audit_method: direct URL fetch — strip-first repair, 2026-06-12. Each retained source URL fetched directly with curl; no WebSearch snippets used.

rows.supporting_quote: Salida Ridge Apartments provides 48 affordable rental housing units to residents of Salida with incomes between 30 to 50 percent of the Area Median Income. Developed by Commonwealth Development Corporation ... reservation of $1,220,000 in federal 9 percent Housing Tax Credits in 2020 and $1,611,000 in permanent financing in 2021.

Confluent Park Apartments, a new 48-unit affordable housing unit, has been approved by the City of Salida ... It will be the first LIHTC development in Salida in more than 20 years. ... 48 units; 24 one-bedroom, 21-two bedroom, and three three-bedroom units.
rows.notes: Direct URL fetch via curl. Original completion, leasing date, $13M cost, and 30%-60% AMI wording were dropped.

rows.supporting_quote: Developments awarded during this round will be located in Aurora, Colorado Springs, Denver, Fort Collins, Glenwood Springs, Granby, Pueblo, and Westminster.
rows.notes: Direct URL fetch via curl; Salida is absent from listed 2024 Round Two locations and project names.

rows.supporting_quote: Developments supported will be located in Alamosa, Aurora, Brighton, Denver, Fort Collins, Frisco, Superior, Trinidad, Westminster, and Wheat Ridge.
rows.notes: Direct URL fetch via curl; Salida is absent from listed 2025 Round One locations and project names.

rows.supporting_quote: 2A – Increases the annual short term rental license to $1,000 for affordable housing ... For 55% | Against 45% ... 2B – Increases lodging tax on short term rentals from 4.82 per day to $15 for affordable housing ... For 51% | Against 47%

rows.supporting_quote: Salida City Council voted 4-1 to reduce fees from a flat rate of $17.40 per square foot ... The new fee structure is as follows ... Dunleavy said the fees in lieu are one of the things that fund the affordable housing fund in the city ... The resolution to lower fees in lieu passed, with Martin dissenting.

rows.supporting_quote: the remaining portion of the original 190-acre parcel ... The City of Salida purchased the 190-acre Vandaveer Ranch near the southeast corner of Salida in 2004 in order to obtain its water rights ... In 2009, the Salida Natural Resource Center Development Corporation (NRCDC), a Colorado nonprofit corporation, was formed to handle financing ... Another reality of the remaining roughly 100-acre parcel still exists ... 50 to 60 acres ... developable

rows.supporting_quote: Ordinance 2023-16, approving a substantial modification to Vandaveer Ranch parcels, including the South Ark Neighborhood ... the master plan ... would allow for up to 400 housing units ... 300, or 75 percent of units, would need to be deed-restricted, with 200, or 50 percent of units, needing to be in the 'affordable' category, and deed-restricted for a minimum of five years.

rows.supporting_quote: Formed in 2020 by Chaffee County, the City of Salida, and the Town of Buena Vista, CHA is a multi-jurisdictional housing authority working across Chaffee County
rows.notes: Direct URL fetch via curl. Board seating date and IHP manager/140% AMI details were dropped.

rows.supporting_quote: Using the Community Land Trust model, we promote responsible community development by providing affordable housing and rentals to qualified low-income residents.
rows.notes: Direct URL fetch via curl. Founding year, 99-year lease, and completed/development counts were dropped.

rows.supporting_quote: 6A — adding a 0.5 percent sales tax ... No/against 7,474 ... 6B — Retaining revenue for affordable housing ... Yes/for 7,057 ... Ballot measure 6A proposed adding a 0.5 percent sales tax ... Ballot measure 6B allows the CHA to keep all of the money they earn ... 6B is a “debrucing” measure
rows.notes: Direct URL fetch via curl. $3.5M/year and 12-year sunset were dropped.

rows.supporting_quote: Unofficial results show Chaffee County voters rejecting ballot measure 1A, with 58.19% voting against the proposed lodging tax increase. Chaffee County's ballot question 1A would have increased the county lodging tax from 1.9% to 5.9%

Jane's Place is a unique, mixed-use development planned for a currently vacant half-acre lot located a half mile from downtown Salida. The development will consist of four buildings that will deliver 17 transitional housing units ... Over the past several years, CHA and the community have secured approximately $5.7 million dollars for construction ... CHA was awarded $1.287 million from DOLA

The four-building property includes 13 units of affordable housing, the Molly House, and Bloom and Brew coffee shop. ... expects the first round of tenants to move in at the start of 2026. ... Molly House ... adds four units ... The construction and development of Jane's Place relied on multiple grants and donors, including a $1.3 million dollar community revitalization grant from the Colorado Office of Economic Development and International Trade
rows.notes: Direct URL fetch via curl. $300K solar and $260K lodging-tax stack claims were dropped.

rows.supporting_quote: Salida City Council (SCC), unanimously voted to approve Ordinance 2023-20, the impact review and major subdivision for the Flour Mill Planned Development (PD) at 6907 CR 105. The two-acre parcel is owned and being developed by ... Rob Gartzman ... 69 housing units ... Fifty percent of units are deed-restricted ... Units are priced at 120 percent AMI, and some for 100 percent AMI
